events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: spring can travel pins not pulled after hydro. This is about spring hangers/supports used in piping. Spring can supports have travel pins that keep the spring locked during installation until the system is hydrotested and operating at normal temperature. After hydro (and typically after the system is at operating condition), the travel pins should be pulled/removed so the spring can function properly. If the travel pins are not pulled after hydro, the spring hanger is not functioning as designed—it's still locked, not supporting the pipe dynamically. This is non-compliant with manufacturer requirements and MSS SP-58.

Decision: fail
Risk: medium (the pipe isn't getting proper support from spring hangers, could lead to stress issues)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 90
Value: 0
Confidence: 0.9
Findings: spring can travel pins not pulled after hydrotest
Actions: pull/remove travel pins per manufacturer instructions after hydrotest
References: MSS SP-58, project support standards, manufacturer requirements
